title: Quiz: authentication vs authorization
prompt_md: Which pair correctly describes the difference between authentication and authorization?
options: Authentication verifies who you are (logging in / proving identity); authorization checks what you are allowed to do (permissions / roles)
Authentication and authorization are two words for the same login step
Authentication encrypts the request body; authorization decrypts it on the server
Authentication checks the user's role; authorization verifies the password at login
explanation_md: **Authentication (authn)** answers *"who are you?"* — it verifies identity, e.g. checking a username/password or validating a **JWT** to log a user in. **Authorization (authz)** answers *"what are you allowed to do?"* — it checks **permissions**, typically via **roles** or **RBAC**, after identity is already established. The order matters: you authenticate first, then authorize each action. A clean one-liner for the interview: *authn proves identity, authz grants access.* Encryption is a separate concern entirely.
